description: Sony, once the titan of portable music, struggled to pivot from its proprietary ATRAC/MiniDisc ecosystem to MP3 players. The 2004 launch of the Sony Network Walkman faced heavy skepticism compared to the rising dominance of the Apple iPod, ultimately resulting in Sony ceding market leadership in digital music.

key_events.description: Sony launches the Network Walkman requiring proprietary SonicStage software.
Tech critics highlight the seamless iTunes integration of Apple's iPod.
Market share data shows iPod accelerating past Sony's portable audio segment.
Sony begins losing significant retail shelf space to competing MP3 manufacturers.

calibration_notes: Excellent for calibrating 'incumbent failure' models where a dominant player loses market share due to user-hostile ecosystem lock-in.
